title: Supreme Court confirms order to dissolve Unification Church
summary: The highest court rejected the church's special appeal and affirmed that the dissolution order for excessive donations does not violate religious freedom.

title: Possible data leak of up to 14.22 million users in KDDI system
summary: The provider reported that unauthorized access to its email system may have exposed addresses and passwords of Biglobe users.

title: Fire at elementary school in Tokyo triggers emergency inspections in other cities
summary: The fire originated near an electric heater with a burnt plug, and led to safety inspections in schools in Kitakyushu.

title: Okinawa commemorates Memorial Day with calls for peace
summary: On the 81st anniversary of the end of the Battle of Okinawa, ceremonies were held in memory of the deceased, with speeches and readings of poems.

title: World Cup records outstanding performances from Messi, Haaland and Mbappé
summary: Several teams secured their place in the round of 16 as stars shine with multiple goals and records are broken in the tournament.

title: Nissan shareholders' meeting rejects reelection of outside director
summary: Shareholders voted against the reelection of Nagai, questioned for his independence, amid criticism from Renault.

title: Nikkei drops more than 2500 points and falls below 70,000
summary: The Japanese stock index suffered a sharp decline due to profit-taking after eight consecutive sessions of gains.

title: Two typhoons approaching Japan: Typhoon 7 could approach Kanto and Typhoon 8 forms heading toward Okinawa
summary: Authorities urge staying informed ahead of the possible arrival of consecutive typhoons and their effects on the archipelago.

title: Proposal to reduce food VAT to 1% for two years sparks debate
summary: The plan of Prime Minister Takai would negatively affect 70% of restaurants, which are seeking alternatives like takeout food.

title: Shizuoka governor approves start of construction of linear train in its section
summary: An agreement with JR Tokai is expected to be signed in July to begin construction of the magnetic Shinkansen in the prefecture.

title: Fair Trade Commission recommends YKK avoid unfair pricing purchases
summary: It was determined that the company paid below the minimum wage of Toyama prefecture for zipper work.

title: Prime Minister Takai will not attend NATO summit for second consecutive year
summary: The decision could reduce Japan's influence in the Atlantic alliance, according to analysts and media.

title: Yen reaches 161 level and intervention alert remains
summary: Finance Minister Katayama met with US Treasury Secretary to discuss the exchange rate situation.

title: North Korea reaffirms nuclear strengthening policy and criticizes Japan as 'war-mongering country'
summary: Kim Jong-un declared in a party meeting that the country will continue developing its nuclear capability to 'overwhelm the world'.

title: United States allows Iranian oil exports and progress made in nuclear negotiations
summary: The US administration temporarily eased sanctions to allow dollar payments, while crude oil prices fall on prospects of progress.

title: Shohei Ohtani hits 17th home run of the season and leads All-Star voting
summary: The Dodgers player continues to accumulate hits and approaches 300 career home runs.

title: Go player Shinichi Takao assumes office as new president of the Japanese Go Association
summary: The former national champion will seek to stabilize the entity's finances and promote the traditional game.

title: Human remains found in closet of an apartment in Osaka
summary: A cleaning worker discovered a skeleton in a vacant home, and police are investigating the case as abandonment of a corpse.

title: Alan Greenspan, former Federal Reserve chairman, passes away
summary: Considered a 'master' of monetary policy, he led the US economy for decades.

title: Debris removal begins at Fukushima Daiichi Unit 1 after hydrogen explosion
summary: After 15 years, cleaning of the debris generated by the 2011 nuclear accident begins.
